sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see what happened. The dark gray frame (node 7, color `c`) around the bottom blue target has been replaced by a white frame (node 7, color `W`, 16 pixels). The blue square (node 8) is still inside it. The player's white part (previously node 9) is now gone - it seems the player merged with the frame or the frame expanded.
Looking at node 7: it's now white (W), 16 pixels, boundary [[36, 32], [36, 35], [40, 35], [40, 32]] - that's a 5x4 area. The blue square is still inside at [[37, 33], [37, 34], [38, 34], [38, 33]].
The player's white part seems to have been absorbed/merged. The green part (node 9) is still at [[41, 32], [41, 35], [43, 35], [43, 32]].
So SPACE converted the dark frame to white. The goal might be to convert all dark frames to white. Let me check - there are 3 dark frames (nodes 1, 3, and the original node 7). Now node 7 is white, but nodes 1 and 3 are still dark.
I need to navigate to the other two dark frames and press SPACE on them too. Let me figure out how to move. The player is now just the green part at rows 41-43, cols 32-35. I need to go to:
- Top-right frame: rows 24-27, cols 44-47
- Left frame: rows 28-31, cols 16-19
Let me try moving around. First, let me check if I can move in any direction.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# SPACE converted the dark frame to white! The goal is likely to convert all dark frames.
# Remaining dark frames: node 1 (top-right) and node 3 (left)
# Player (green) at rows 41-43, cols 32-35
# Need to navigate to other frames

# Let me try moving DOWN first to see if player can move
action(['DOWN'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
